title: Error while running podman machine start
body:
### Issue Description

While running podman machine start getting below error.

podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: -virtfs local,path=/scratch/obaas,mount_tag=vol0,security_model=none: 'virtio-9p-pci' is not a valid device model name

# podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: -virtfs local,path=/scratch/obaas,mount_tag=vol0,security_model=none: 'virtio-9p-pci' is not a valid device model name

comments.body:
Is there a reason you are trying to run `podman machine` on linux? You can just use podman natively without VM on linux.

Regardless this looks like your qemu install does not support 9p mounts so it does not look like a podman problem.

I am trying to run minikube on podman 
Following the doc https://minikube.sigs.k8s.io/docs/drivers/podman/

Where I can get the help for qemu ?
I have installed qemu-kvm 
dnf info qemu-kvm
Last metadata expiration check: 1 day, 2:16:52 ago on Wed 11 Oct 2023 09:17:32 AM GMT.
Installed Packages
Name         : qemu-kvm
Epoch        : 15
Version      : 6.2.0
Release      : 33.module+el8.8.0+21164+ed375313
Architecture : x86_64
Size         : 0.0  
Source       : qemu-kvm-6.2.0-33.module+el8.8.0+21164+ed375313.src.rpm
Repository   : @System
From repo    : ol8_appstream
Summary      : QEMU is a machine emulator and virtualizer
URL          : http://www.qemu.org/
License      : GPLv2 and GPLv2+ and CC-BY
Description  : qemu-kvm is an open source virtualizer that provides hardware
             : emulation for the KVM hypervisor. qemu-kvm acts as a virtual
             : machine monitor together with the KVM kernel modules, and emulates the
             : hardware for a full system such as a PC and its associated peripherals.

> I am trying to run minikube on podman
Following the doc https://minikube.sigs.k8s.io/docs/drivers/podman/

there it says:
> On all other operating systems, make sure to create and start the virtual machine that is needed for Podman.

This instruction does not apply to linux, you can use podman natively without the machine.

If you use distros shipping old versions is it always recommend to go through the distro support/bug channels for help not upstream.
